title: stevia obovata var. obovata
intro: Stevia obovata var. obovata, a perennial shrub native to the Andes mountains in South America, is a member of the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is also known as "Andean sweetleaf" or "stevia". It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce blood pressure and improve glucose tolerance, due to its high concentration of steviosides, a group of sweet-tasting compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing inflammation, and improving oral health. Stevia obovata var. obovata is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
